title: 10 best herbal creams for chills

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with chills because they stimulate blood circulation, which helps to warm the body from within. The warming properties of ginger also help to relax muscles, reducing shivering and discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce fever and relieve congestion, further alleviating symptoms of chill. By using herbal ginger creams, individuals can quickly and effectively alleviate the uncomfortable sensations caused by chills, promoting overall well-being and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as muscle soreness and joint pain.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, cause blood vessels to dilate, which helps increase blood flow to the skin, helping to warm the body up and reduce chills.

Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial agents have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections, which may contribute to chills and fever; by combating these microorganisms, herbal ginger creams help promote a healthy recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal creams for chills because they contain natural oils and compounds that help to soothe and warm the skin.
Roots: Roots are a popular choice for medicinal creams for chills due to their ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation, helping to relieve symptoms of chilliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and blend them in a food processor with 1 cup of coconut oil until you get a smooth paste.
Transfer the paste to a double boiler and simmer it over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible, then discard the solids.
Allow the herbal infusion to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container and storing it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with chills because of its natural calming properties. The herb, Melissa officinalis, has been used for centuries to soothe nervous system imbalances, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and shivering. When applied topically in a cream form, the oils from lemon balm are absorbed into the skin, helping to calm and warm the body from the outside in. This natural remedy can provide rapid relief from chills and promote overall relaxation and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat viral infections that can cause chills by reducing the replication of viruses and alleviating symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral, reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects, attributed to its flavonoids and terpenes, help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, which help to soothe and calm the body, reducing symptoms of chill.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in essential oils that provide anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate chill-related discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at peak potency in summer mornings after dew.
Dry the fresh leaves using a dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Steep 10 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids before mixing with beeswax and shea butter at a ratio of 1:2.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, then pour into glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with chills because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to soothe and calm the body. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of lemongrass help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, while its warming effects help to stimulate blood circulation and promote a sense of relaxation. As a result, herbal lemongrass creams can provide fast and effective relief from chills, helping to restore body heat and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cytokine Modulation: Lemongrass cream's cytokine-modulating property helps reduce inflammation and fever, alleviating chill symptoms by regulating the body's response to infection or injury.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of lemongrass cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which contributes to chills, pain, and discomfort during illness.

Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lemongrass cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help relieve body aches, joint pain, and stiffness associated with chills, making it an effective natural remedy for symptom relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce body temperature and alleviate chill symptoms.
Buds: Lemongrass buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have analgesic and anti-pyretic properties, making them effective in soothing chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and rinse them with distilled water.
Steep the cymbopogon citratus leaves in 200ml of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix 100g of unscented coconut oil with 50ml of the cymbopogon citratus infusion and heat gently until dissolved.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel inside the aloe vera plant contains compounds that help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chills. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds can quickly calm the skin, reducing feelings of coldness and warmth while also providing long-lasting relief from itching and burning sensations. As a result, herbal aloe creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating chills and promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever and chills, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated skin.

Antimicrobial: Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infections and exacerbate chill symptoms, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.
Soothing and cooling: Aloe vera's gel has a calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce heat and discomfort caused by chills, and providing relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of aloin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing chill symptoms.
Roots: Containing a range of bioactive compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helping to combat chill-related infections.
remedy_recipe: Collect and clean 50-70 grams of fresh aloe vera leaves rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Extract the gel from the leaves using a vegetable peeler or a sharp knife remove any remaining green skin.
Mix one cup of distilled water with one tablespoon of glycerin add three tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the extracted aloe vera gel to the mixture stir well until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 20-25 minutes pour into containers store in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with chills because they contain calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the skin. When applied topically, it helps to reduce heat and discomfort associated with fever or chill. The cream's cooling properties also help to ease muscle tension and relaxation, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. Additionally, pot marigold creams contain antioxidants that help to protect the skin from further irritation and inflammation, making them an effective natural remedy for chills.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold creams contain antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate chills by reducing body temperature and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial compounds, such as saponins and terpenes, help combat infections that can cause chills, promoting a healthy immune response and reducing fever.

Soften and moisturize skin properties: Pot marigold creams' emollient properties soothe and soften the skin, helping to reduce dryness and irritation that can exacerbate chill symptoms like shivering and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, the roots help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle cramps associated with chills.
Flowers: The flowers' antiseptic properties make them effective in preventing infection and promoting wound healing when applied topically as a cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine 200g of dried calendula petals with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 hours at room temperature or overnight in the refrigerator.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean container discarding the solids after pressing gently.
Mix the infused oil with 200g of beeswax and heat slowly over low heat stirring occasionally until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with chills because of its unique blend of natural ingredients, including Echinacea angustifolia, which has been traditionally used to combat various health issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties help to soothe and calm the body, reducing fever and alleviating chill-related discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream help to keep the skin hydrated, further supporting the body's natural defense against infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as triterpenoids, which can help reduce body temperature and alleviate chill symptoms by decreasing inflammation in the body.

Antiviral activity: Kansas coneflower has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that may be causing chills, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
Immune system support: The cream contains bioactive compounds that can help stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and reduce the occurrence of chill symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the skin, making them an excellent choice for treating chill-induced skin irritations.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Kansas coneflower have antiviral properties that help combat viral infections, such as those that cause chills, and provide relief from symptoms like fever and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried echinacea angustifolia flowers in bulk according to your supplier's recommended guidelines for quality control and storage.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for optimal extraction efficiency.
Combine 20 grams of powdered echinacea angustifolia with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the clear liquid for further processing.

Blend the reserved infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler, stirring constantly until fully melted and cooled to form a smooth, spreadable cream within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with chills because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the skin and reduce discomfort. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion root extract, work to calm the body's response to chills, alleviating symptoms like shivering and stiffness. Additionally, the cream's warming and moisturizing effects help to restore skin health, providing long-lasting relief from chills. remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by chills.
Antibacterial and antiviral activity: The presence of antimicrobial compounds in dandelion cream helps to combat bacterial and viral infections that may be contributing to the onset of chills.

Warming and soothing properties: Dandelion's warming properties help to increase blood flow, reducing feelings of coldness and numbness associated with chills, while its soothing effects calm irritated skin and relieve discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing chill symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing chilliness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves, carefully selecting those free from pesticides or contaminants for use in the recipe.
Combine one part dried taraxacum officinale herb with three parts olive oil and four parts coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler over simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the oils infuse with the herbal extract for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for use in the cream recipe.
Mix one part of the infused oil with two parts beeswax, three parts shea butter, and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil, heating the mixture over low heat until the ingredients melt and combine smoothly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with chills because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which help to relax muscle spasms and calm the body. When applied topically, chamomile cream can soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation, providing fast relief from cold-related symptoms like shivering and goosebumps. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams can reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate chill symptoms such as fever, swelling, and pain, by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in chamomile cream help combat infections that may be causing chills, such as the common cold or flu, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.

Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile creams can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to chills, and improve overall sleep quality, helping the body to recover from illness more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm the body's response to chills.
Flowers: The delicate petals of the chamomile flower contain apigenin, an antioxidant that promotes relaxation and reduces fever.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100 grams of beeswax pellets into a double boiler for melting.
Combine 200 milliliters of distilled water and 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in a heatproof bowl.
Simmer the herbal mixture for 10 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into another bowl.
Melt the beeswax pellets with 150 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 grams of coconut oil in the double boiler.
Combine the melted wax mixture with the cooled herbal infusion and stir until it thickens to a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to increase body temperature and alleviate symptoms of cold and flu. The creams contain compounds like anethole, which has a warming effect on the skin, increasing blood flow and circulation. This can help to dissipate heat more efficiently, reducing feelings of chilliness and discomfort. Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic effects calm muscle cramps, spasms, and contractions caused by chills, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and coughs associated with chills, and making breathing easier.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating body aches associated with chills.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural warming properties, which can help to increase circulation and relieve cold feelings in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems then wash thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the herb for 30 minutes to extract its essential oil and separate it from plant material.
Combine 10 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare powder with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it to sterilized glass containers for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice creams helps with chills because of its unique blend of natural ingredients, including herbs and spices that work together to provide soothing relief from cold discomfort. The warmth and comfort provided by allspice creams help to alleviate chilliness, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties in allspice creams may also help to reduce swelling and redness associated with chills, further contributing to overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Allspice cream's antipyretic properties help to reduce fever and alleviate chills by counteracting the underlying inflammatory response.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in allspice cream, such as Eugenol and Cinnamic acid, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness.

Warmth-inducing properties: Allspice contains compounds that have a warming effect on the body, such as Cinnamaldehyde, which helps to increase blood flow and warmth, making it feel more comfortable when experiencing chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing body temperature and alleviating chill symptoms.
Barks: Contain astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, which can aid in warming the body and reducing shivering.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, including camphor and eugenol, which provide natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 2 tablespoons of fresh pimenta dioica leaves in the shade for at least 48 hours.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried pimenta dioica leaves with 4 ounces of coconut oil and heat the mixture for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for 8-12 hours or overnight to allow the herbs to steep.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Add 2 teaspoons of beeswax pellets and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and stir until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with chills because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the skin. The aroma of lavender has a profound effect on the body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. When applied topically as a cream, it helps to warm and comfort the skin, easing feelings of shivering and chilliness. Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever or flu-like symptoms, making it an effective remedy for soothing aching muscles and joints.
Anxiolytic property: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by chills, promoting relaxation and improving overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are used to create a calming and soothing effect in medicinal creams, helping to reduce chill symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Combine one cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with two cups of water and bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and let it cool completely.
Add one cup of distilled water and two tablespoons of beeswax to the cooled liquid and gently warm the mixture over low heat stirring until the wax is fully melted.
Remove from heat and let it cool and thicken for about an hour before transferring to an airtight container for storage and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with chills because of its natural ability to warm and soothe the skin. The cream's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat infection and reduce inflammation, while its emollient qualities provide a calming and comforting sensation. As a result, damask rose creams can effectively alleviate chills by promoting warmth and relaxation, helping to restore balance to the body's temperature regulation system and promote overall well-being.

Antimicrobial property: The creams contain antimicrobial agents like phenolic acids and terpenes, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms that can cause chills and fevers, promoting a healthy body response to illness.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing fever and soothing the skin.
Buds: The buds are rich in flavonoids, which have natural antiseptic properties that help combat infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried rosa damascena petals and store them in an airtight container for future use.
Combine 100 grams of dried rosa damascena petals with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil at a temperature of 60 to 70 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids, then add 2 teaspoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture, then fill into jars and store in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with chills because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that work together to provide relief from shivering and body aches. The cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles, while its anti-inflammatory agents reduce swelling and ease pain. remedy_properties: Antiviral Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream contains eucalyptol, which has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against several viruses, helping to combat chill-inducing pathogens and reduce symptoms.

Decongestant Properties: The menthol content in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream helps to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure associated with chills, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Eucalyptus oil in the cream has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with chill-induced respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or pneumonia.

Barks: Containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, barks provide antimicrobial properties that aid in treating infections contributing to chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the eucalyptus powder with 100ml of distilled water in a glass bowl, allowing it to steep for 15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing gently to extract the maximum amount of herbal essence and discarding the solids after 10 minutes.

Combine the filtered eucalyptus extract with 200g of unsalted butter and 50ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating it under low heat for 30 minutes until the mixture is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with chills because of its unique ability to soothe and warm the skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata resin extract from mature trees during peak resin production season.
Mix 200 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Add 20 grams of boswellia serrata powder to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature over 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy consistency.
Transfer the final cream to sterilized jars and seal with tight-fitting lids for storage and dispensing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried geranium maculatum flowers at 50 grams for each 100 milliliters of cream base.
Combine the dried flowers with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for a 24-hour infusion time.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and combine it with 80 grams of beeswax for melting at 170 degrees fahrenheit.
Whip the mixture until smooth, adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for a calming effect and 100 milliliters of distilled water to achieve the desired consistency.
Fill clean glass jars with the geranium maculatum cream and store them in an airtight container for up to six months at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves then dry them properly for two weeks.
Combine dried flowers and leaves with jojoba oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Steep mixture for 30 minutes under low heat before straining it through cheesecloth.
Mix strained infusion with vitamin e oil and coconut oil then whip until smooth.
Pour whipped cream into sterilized jars sealing them tightly within five minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1/4 cup of olive oil in a clean glass jar and store it at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the strained liquid and heat it in a double boiler until the wax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with chills because of its natural warming properties, which can help to stimulate blood flow and relax muscles. The herbs in the cream, such as Origanum majorana, contain compounds that have a soothing effect on the skin and can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with colds and flu. Additionally, the aromatic oils present in wild marjoram can be invigorating and uplifting, helping to promote feelings of warmth and comfort when applied topically.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wild marjoram's antispasmodic compounds help to relax muscles, relieve spasms, and ease cramps, which can help alleviate chill-induced muscle tension.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of wild marjoram cream can help thin and loosen mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, and providing relief from chills and congestion.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram's antiseptic and antimicrobial compounds help to protect against infections, reducing the risk of secondary illnesses that can arise from being chilled.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming the body during chills.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce fever and alleviate chill symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh origanum vulgare leaves and stems, cleaning them thoroughly with water.
Combine the herbs with 1 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining it through cheesecloth.
Mix the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into sterile tubes or jars, allowing to solidify completely at room temperature within 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with chills because of its natural warming and soothing properties. The herb thyme contains compounds such as thymol, which has been traditionally used to relieve cold-induced chills. When applied topically in a cream form, thyme's warmth and calming effects help to counteract the sensations of chilliness, providing rapid relief from shivering and discomfort. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it also helps to promote blood flow and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its effectiveness in combatting chills.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains compounds like carvacrol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of chills such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Antiviral properties: Thymol has also been found to have antiviral activity against certain viruses, including those that may be causing chills in the first place, helping to shorten the duration and severity of the illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in essential oils that help to stimulate blood circulation and warm the body, making them effective in relieving chills.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and fever associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested thyme leaves with 120 milliliters of olive oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Melt 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler while stirring occasionally over low heat until fully liquefied after 10 minutes.
Combine the infused thyme oil with the melted beeswax and stir until well combined then pour into sterilized jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for chills are topical preparations that combine natural herbs and essential oils to provide relief from chills, fever, and body aches. These creams help by warming the skin, relaxing muscles, and reducing inflammation. Examples of herbal creams that can help with chills include those infused with peppermint, ginger, and eucalyptus oil. These creams can improve lives by providing fast and effective relief from cold and flu symptoms, allowing individuals to feel comfortable and resume their daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chills with herbal creams is by applying a topical cream containing warming herbs such as ginger, cinnamon, or capsaicin. These herbs stimulate blood flow, relax muscles, and warm the skin, helping to alleviate chilliness and discomfort. Simply apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area and gently massage until absorbed for effective relief from chills.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal creams are arthritis, muscle soreness, and inflammation. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort can help alleviate symptoms like joint pain, stiffness, and swelling associated with these conditions. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may provide relief from discomfort and promote healing.
